title: EX-0004 final animation and generated-art polish
goal: Finish the owner-approved EX-0004 pond-board exercise with purposeful frog/trace/water motion and the committed generated pond atmosphere while preserving the exact orthogonal unit geometry, interaction loop, accessibility, and unbranded original presentation.
acceptance: The committed generated pond image is integrated only as replaceable atmosphere or framing; it carries no required state, answer, target quantity, readable text, logo, trademark, copied character, or branding, and it never introduces a competing perspective grid into the active mathematical corridor.

Purposeful motion makes frog takeoff, one-unit landing, two-unit obstacle leap, retained trace, discrepancy, and exact arrival easier to read; motion does not add timing skill or delay input unnecessarily.

Every active movement cell remains a true rendered square; ordinary horizontal and vertical displacement remains exactly one 10-unit cell side and the obstacle leap remains exactly two unit lengths in one action.

Reduced-motion mode preserves every final landing, trace, discrepancy, outcome label, focus transition, and completion path without required animation; keyboard and 320px touch paths remain equivalent.
Intro, all four scenes, retry-in-place behavior, success, and completion retain existing deterministic smoke hooks and no stale animation callback can mutate a later scene.

DESIGN.md and asset provenance record the generated-art role, no-branding review, safe crop, motion rationale, desktop/320px review, and technical evidence without claiming learning efficacy or MES-02 approval.
must_ask_if: The validated prediction, automatic-resolution, orthogonal-transfer, obstacle arithmetic, retry, or progression semantics would need to change.
The generated image cannot be integrated without making its perspective grid look like the playable unit grid or obscuring mathematical state.
A copied branded character, logo, title treatment, trademark, layout, or recognizable source composition would remain visible.
A runtime dependency, network asset, or file outside the declared EX-0004 directory appears necessary.
